title: Evil as Relational Closure
one_sentence: This module defines evil as persistent closure of relational futures under irreversibility.

core_compression.evil_not_error: persistent closure that makes recovery cost explode

closure_conditions.E1: closure appears as stability or relief

corollaries.intention_free: optimization, bureaucracy, and algorithmic bias can produce closure without malicious intent
corollaries.good_intention: closure can be chosen to save locally while destroying relational futures
corollaries.seductive: closure can feel attractive because it behaves like a strong attractor

directional_dual.ethics: keeps Omega_rel open through validation and commitment
directional_dual.evil: closes Omega_rel by suppressing variance and exploiting commitment

negative_constraints: Do not treat evil as a moral label for agents.
Do not classify persons or groups as evil.
Do not use this module as theology, legal guilt, punishment theory, or moral condemnation.
